# aeon-justice-geometric-fractal-2026-04-21

This note critiques an essay on geometric/fractal systems for potentially romanticizing decentralized models without addressing real failure modes like free-riders and information asymmetry across scales. The user proposes testing whether "circulation vs extraction" framing has actual predictive power by modifying their commitment ledger schema and measuring whether adoption reduces the 75% abandonment rate over 30 days. Success would validate fractal/circulation as load-bearing for system design; failure would suggest the framing is decorative and a different axis explains the dysfunction.
